title: New buyer credit limit policy
text: First-time buyers (is_new_buyer=true) are issued an initial credit limit of TWD 10,000 to TWD 20,000 depending on JCIC score band. Limits above TWD 30,000 require six months of clean repayment history. New-buyer cohort baseline delinquency is approximately 5.5% versus 4.5% for returning buyers.

title: Returning buyer credit limit policy
text: Returning buyers (is_new_buyer=false) with 12+ months of payment history qualify for limits up to TWD 80,000. Limit increases are reviewed quarterly. Returning buyers exhibit lower delinquency (~4.5%) and longer days_late tails.

title: Travel merchant segment risk premium
text: Travel-segment merchants carry a baseline delinquency premium of +1.0 percentage points versus the portfolio average. This reflects longer fulfilment cycles and seasonal cancellation patterns. Travel-segment new-buyer credit limits are capped at TWD 30,000 until two clean cycles complete.

title: Gaming merchant segment cap policy
text: Gaming-segment transactions are capped at TWD 5,000 per order for new buyers and TWD 15,000 for returning buyers. Daily cumulative cap is TWD 30,000 across all gaming merchants per customer.

title: Retail merchant segment baseline
text: Retail is the reference segment for risk modelling. No segment-level risk premium applied. Retail accounts for approximately 37% of portfolio transaction volume.

title: Data dictionary: is_delinquent
text: is_delinquent is a boolean field set to true when a customer's payment is overdue by 7 or more days past the invoice due date. Once flagged, the record stays delinquent until the balance settles or is written off at 90 days.

title: Data dictionary: days_late bucket definitions
text: days_late uses standard buckets: 7, 14, 30, 45, 60, 90. 7-14 days = early-stage (collections call); 30-45 = mid-stage (formal demand); 60-90 = late-stage (write-off candidate); 90+ = charged off.

title: Data dictionary: merchant_segment categories
text: merchant_segment takes three values: travel, gaming, retail. Segment is assigned by merchant onboarding and does not change per transaction. Segment-level parameter adjustments are reviewed quarterly by the risk team.

title: Data dictionary: quarter field
text: quarter takes Q1-Q4 based on transaction_date. Q3 historically shows the highest delinquency rate due to summer travel volume and back-to-school spending. Q3 new-buyer delinquency typically runs 1.5-2.0 percentage points above the annual average.

title: Data dictionary: device_type
text: device_type is one of ios, android, web. Mobile (ios+android) accounts for ~70% of order volume. Web orders skew toward higher order_value_twd. No device-level risk weighting currently applied.

title: Quarterly delinquency review playbook
text: Risk team runs a quarterly review covering: (1) overall unpaid rate vs 3.5% target, (2) segment-level delinquency deltas, (3) new-buyer cohort discrimination (Q1->Q4 lift). Findings feed into the parameter-adjustment proposal for the next quarter.

title: Unpaid rate target and intervention
text: Portfolio-wide unpaid rate target is 3.5%. The current intervention rule-set (NOT a model) is applied at order time and contributed to moving unpaid rate from 4.5% to 3.5% — approximately 22% relative reduction. Rules cover order-value ceilings, segment-specific caps, and JCIC-fallback triggers.

title: Quarterly spike investigation SOP
text: When a quarter's delinquency rate exceeds the trailing 4-quarter average by more than 1.0 percentage points, run the spike SOP: (1) decompose by is_new_buyer and merchant_segment, (2) check order_value distribution shift, (3) cross-reference external data vendor signals, (4) propose parameter adjustment within 5 business days. Q3 spikes are usually new-buyer + travel driven.

title: JCIC fallback policy
text: When JCIC (Joint Credit Information Center) returns no record for an applicant — typical for under-25 first-time buyers — fall back to the alternative-data scoring pipeline (telecom + device + e-invoice + third-party behavioural). Initial limit is capped at TWD 15,000 regardless of segment.

title: External data vendor catalog
text: Four categories of alternative data are integrated via a unified vendor API: telecom (mobile contract tenure, billing record), device (device fingerprint, OS age), e-invoice (Ministry of Finance issuance signal), third-party behavioural (e-commerce repayment history). POC validates discrimination lift on the no-JCIC subset; productionization is in progress.
